steps.title: Find where the sophist reveals his exaggerated sense of wisdom
steps.content: We need a quotation where a sophist directly shows — through his own words or expectations — that he overestimates his own wisdom.

steps.title: Identify the sophist's self-assessment
steps.content: The correct answer has Socrates expecting students to "seize immediately" his "clever dogma" — his own words reveal how highly he regards his wisdom and how easily he assumes others will grasp it. This directly shows exaggerated self-assessment.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• "You talk nonsense. Begone" shows a sophist dismissing an opponent's view. Dismissal of others is not the same as overestimating one's own wisdom; it does not reveal how highly the sophist rates himself. Eliminated.
  • "These men teach" describes what sophists teach or the content of their lessons. Describing a teaching topic says nothing about whether the sophist thinks too highly of his own expertise. Eliminated.
  • "Go... and be taught" shows a student being encouraged to learn. Encouragement reflects the speaker's attitude toward the student, not an inflated assessment of the sophist's own wisdom. Eliminated.
The correct answer is the only quotation where the sophist's own words betray an exaggerated view of his wisdom — D is correct.
